handoff_meta.purpose: Content-phase planning + system state for ChatGPT guidance
handoff_meta.authoring_note: Prepared after Stage 14 runtime async hardening and full-suite validation.

high_level_summary: Gateway command translation exists for read/start/equip/unequip/dungeon lifecycle/active commands.
Canonical runtime path is wired and now async-safe (eventBus + orchestrator + runtime command processing).
Persistence boundaries exist for character/inventory/session/combat with inMemory + sqlite adapters.
Subsystem harnesses exist for combat, dungeon, loot, economy, crafting, and runtime loop.
System is entering content-heavy phase; combat design can be incrementally improved or rewritten behind adapters.

must_keep: World, Session, Combat state remain separate
Inventory mutation goes through canonical inventory boundary

implemented: safe fallback reply handling when runtime/controller throws
notes: Gateway now forwards command events into canonical runtime path.
Ping remains a shell command path for bot connectivity checks.

implemented: event router with explicit unknown-event failure
recent_hardening: eventBus publish supports sync and async handlers
orchestrator run awaits publish and preserves deterministic queue ordering
gateway awaits runtime processing and sends safe fallback reply on exceptions

combat_system.current_assessment: feature-rich scaffold; good candidate for selective cleanup or full v2 combat core behind same event contracts

implemented: dungeon harness + first playable loop harness

character: adapters to combat and dungeon shapes

recommended_content_principles: Do not add new gameplay mutation logic to gateway/controller.
Content should be data-driven where possible (tables/profiles/templates).
Preserve event names and payload shape stability where already wired.
Prefer adding content packs over touching runtime plumbing.
Use harnesses to validate content behavior before Discord exposure.

combat_redo_strategy_options.when_to_choose: If current combat behavior is mostly acceptable and timelines are tight.
steps: keep existing tests green while adding behavior snapshots

combat_redo_strategy_options.option: B - parallel v2 combat core behind adapter
combat_redo_strategy_options.when_to_choose: If current combat needs structural redesign but you want safe migration.
steps: adapter maps existing events to v2
flip feature flag once parity reached

combat_redo_strategy_options.when_to_choose: Only if speed now matters more than migration safety.
combat_redo_strategy_options.warning: Can destabilize current Stage 14 command/runtime integration.

known_decisions_for_content_phase.recommended_choice: B - parallel v2 combat core behind adapter

phase_1_stabilize_contracts: Generate one canonical event map doc (command -> event_type -> target_system -> handler entry).
Add contract tests that fail on event shape drift.
phase_2_content_scaffold: Create data folders for enemies, encounters, dungeons, loot packs, items, classes/races/backgrounds metadata.
Add lightweight loaders/validators (no gameplay logic) for content JSON.
Add deterministic seed utilities for encounter/loot content testing.
phase_3_combat_redo_prep: Define combat v2 boundary interface and adapter contract.
Identify minimal combat v2 MVP: turn state, action validation, resolution order, persistence points.
Build parity tests against key Stage 14 action flows.
phase_4_runtime_integration: Route content-driven encounter generation through existing event chain.
Add snapshot replay tests for dungeon + combat continuity.
phase_5_release_guardrails: Use test:stage14 as mandatory pre-merge gate.
Run harness suites against both inMemory and sqlite where feasible.
Add one smoke script for gateway command to runtime to persistence path.

current_risks_and_watchlist: Controller handlers file is large and highly coupled; consider domain handler registration map for maintainability.
Runtime logs include expected error output from throw tests; keep this intentional and documented.
Content growth can create schema drift; add strict content validators early.
Combat complexity may outpace maintainability without a v2 boundary plan.
chatgpt_seed_prompt: Use this handoff as the working baseline. Keep event-driven boundaries strict, avoid gameplay logic in gateway/controller/runtime plumbing, prioritize data-driven content scaffolding, and propose a combat-v2 migration path behind stable event contracts.
